blocks.body: Gerund vs Infinitive shows how the form after the verb affects both the grammar and the meaning of the sentence.

{"level":"B1","intro":"In this lesson you will compare two key patterns - verb + gerund and verb + to-infinitive - and see when the difference is grammatical and when it changes the meaning itself.","rules":[{"label":"Different verbs, different patterns","color":"emerald","text":"Some verbs naturally take only one pattern: enjoy doing, but want to do.","example":"enjoy reading / want to read"},{"label":"Sometimes both are possible","color":"blue","text":"There are verbs that allow both forms, but the meaning may change or become more general or more specific.","example":"remember locking / remember to lock"},{"label":"Learn the pair with an example","color":"amber","text":"Here it is important to learn not only the rule but the whole pair with examples, because the difference is often semantic.","example":"stop smoking / stop to smoke"}]}

{"title":"1. Key contrasts","intro":"See how a change of form can change the meaning.","rows":[{"en":"like reading / like to read","ua":"both forms are possible, but one often sounds like a general preferred activity and the other more specific or habitual","note":"I like reading before bed. / I like to read before bed."},{"en":"remember doing / remember to do","ua":"remember a past action / not forget to do something","note":"I remember meeting her. / Remember to call her."},{"en":"stop doing / stop to do","ua":"end one action / stop in order to do another action","note":"He stopped smoking. / He stopped to smoke."},{"en":"try doing / try to do","ua":"test a solution / make an effort to reach a result","note":"Try restarting the app. / Try to finish today."}],"warning":"⚠ You cannot switch -ing and to + verb mechanically: sometimes it changes the meaning, and sometimes it creates an error."}

{"title":"2. How to read the difference","sections":[{"label":"When the pattern is fixed","color":"emerald","description":"With some verbs, the choice of form is simply part of the pattern: enjoy doing, but decide to do.","examples":[{"en":"She avoids talking about politics.","ua":"She avoids talking about politics."},{"en":"We decided to leave early.","ua":"We decided to leave early."},{"en":"They hope to move next year.","ua":"They hope to move next year."}]},{"label":"When both forms are possible","color":"blue","description":"After verbs like like, love, hate, prefer, start, both forms are often possible, but the shade may be more general or more specific.","examples":[{"en":"I like reading in silence.","ua":"I like reading in silence."},{"en":"I like to read the news in the morning.","ua":"I like to read the news in the morning."},{"en":"She started talking again.","ua":"She started talking again."}],"note":"In such cases the difference is not always dramatic, but context still matters."},{"label":"When the meaning changes","color":"amber","description":"With some verbs, the other form gives a different meaning, not just a different grammar shape.","examples":[{"en":"I remembered locking the door.","ua":"I remembered locking the door."},{"en":"I remembered to lock the door.","ua":"I remembered to lock the door."},{"en":"Try changing the password first.","ua":"Try changing the password first."}],"note":"These are the pairs that need separate memorization."}]}

{"title":"3. Common mistakes","items":[{"label":"Mistake 1","color":"rose","title":"Changing the form mechanically without checking the meaning.","wrong":"I stopped to smoke last year.","right":"✅ I stopped smoking last year.","hint":"Here you want to say the habit ended, not that the person paused for a cigarette."},{"label":"Mistake 2","color":"amber","title":"Mixing up memory of an action with the duty to do it.","wrong":"I remembered sending the email, so I wrote it and clicked send.","right":"✅ I remembered to send the email, so I wrote it and clicked send.","hint":"Remember to do = not forget to perform the action."},{"label":"Mistake 3","color":"sky","title":"Ignoring the fixed pattern after a verb.","wrong":"She suggested to meet later.","right":"✅ She suggested meeting later.","hint":"After suggest, the usual pattern is a gerund, not a to-infinitive."}]}

{"title":"4. Quick takeaway","items":["Not all verbs give you a choice: some take only a gerund, others only a to-infinitive.","After verbs like like, love, prefer, start, both forms are often possible, but the shade can change.","After stop, remember, forget, try, regret, the form often changes the meaning itself.","If the pattern looks uncertain, check not only the grammar but also what exactly you want to say.","The best strategy is to learn contrast pairs together with short examples."]}
